name: Wyoming Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Wyoming - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: Wyoming Board of Dietetics, 122 West 25th Street, Suite 106, Herschler Building, Cheyenne, WY 82002

requiredDocuments.name: Official Application Packet
requiredDocuments.description: Application accurately filled out along with proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration sent directly from the CDR.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Wyoming dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is $200.

mainEntity.name: When does the Wyoming license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Wyoming dietitian licenses expire biennially. The renewal fee is $200.

mainEntity.name: Is licensure required to practice in Wyoming?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Wyoming has a Practice Act that legally mandates obtaining a state license to practice dietetics and to use the protected title of Dietitian.

mainEntity.name: Does Wyoming have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Wyoming has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Wyoming?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 1 to 2 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Wyoming while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
